name: claude-win11-speckit-update-skill
capabilities: Provide guidance for Windows 11 system management
Offer patterns for system administration tasks
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis detected 2 low-risk findings (GitHub URLs in metadata) and 2 false positive high-risk findings (non-existent cryptographic patterns). The skill contains no executable code, network operations, file system access, or security vulnerabilities. All findings were determined to be false positives. The skill is a minimal template with only documentation and metadata.
valueStatement: Get guidance for Windows 11 system management tasks. This skill provides patterns and best practices for Windows 11 administration using Claude Code.
limitations: This is a guidance-only skill with no executable automation
Does not perform direct system operations or modifications
Requires manual implementation of provided patterns
Limited to documentation and advisory capabilities
No integration with Windows system APIs

useCases.title: System Administration Planning
useCases.description: Plan Windows 11 system management tasks with documented patterns and best practices

useCases.title: Development Workflow Guidance
useCases.description: Get guidance on Windows 11 development environment setup and management patterns

useCases.title: Learning Windows Management
useCases.description: Learn Windows 11 system management patterns and approaches through documented examples

promptTemplates.title: Basic System Guidance
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the best practices for managing Windows 11 systems?
promptTemplates.scenario: Getting started with Windows 11 system management

promptTemplates.title: Specific Task Pattern
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me the recommended pattern for [specific Windows 11 management task]
promptTemplates.scenario: Requesting guidance on a particular system management activity

promptTemplates.title: Troubleshooting Approach
promptTemplates.prompt: How should I approach troubleshooting [Windows 11 system issue] using standard patterns?
promptTemplates.scenario: Diagnosing and resolving Windows 11 system issues

promptTemplates.title: Advanced Configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the advanced configuration patterns for [Windows 11 system component]?

bestPractices: Always test system management patterns in a non-production environment before production deployment
Document all system changes and maintain a change management log
Use Windows PowerShell or Windows Admin Center for automated system management tasks
antiPatterns: Making undocumented system changes without testing or rollback plans
Applying system management patterns without understanding their dependencies
Skipping backup and recovery procedures before system modifications

faq.question: What Windows 11 management tasks does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill provides guidance and patterns for Windows 11 system management including configuration, administration, and maintenance tasks.

faq.question: Does this skill perform automated system operations?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides guidance and patterns only. It does not execute automated system operations or modifications.

faq.question: Can this skill modify my Windows 11 system settings?
faq.answer: No, this skill is documentation-only. You must manually implement any patterns or guidance it provides.

faq.question: What tools work with this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for Windows 11 system management guidance.

faq.question: Are the patterns provided specific to Windows 11?
faq.answer: Yes, the patterns and guidance are specifically designed for Windows 11 system management.

faq.question: Do I need administrator privileges to use this skill?
faq.answer: The skill itself requires no special privileges. However, implementing Windows 11 management patterns may require administrator access.
